# PRD: Edify OS Landing Page Content Overhaul

**Generated by:** Milo
**Date:** 2026-04-12
**Status:** Draft
**Requested by:** Z

---

## 1. Goal

Replace all generic Spial template text with real Edify OS content, create separate pages for each nav item, and build out a demo/preview of the nonprofit user portal -- all while keeping the Spial template design aesthetic.

## 2. Why

The live site at edifyos.vercel.app is a 1:1 clone of the Spial template with zero mention of nonprofits, AI agents, or what Edify OS actually does. Z can't pitch this to anyone.

Edify OS is built by **Edify** (edifyanother.com), a creative impact studio in Beaufort, SC that specializes in cinematic storytelling for nonprofits. Through years of working with nonprofits, Edify has seen the same pattern: team members wearing too many hats, restricted grant funding that prevents scaling operations, and organizations drowning in execution instead of leading. Edify OS is the tech product extension of that insight -- AI-powered team members that fill the operational gaps nonprofits can't afford to staff.

## 3. Success Criteria

- [ ] Landing page clearly communicates Edify OS's value prop for nonprofits
- [ ] All 7 AI team members presented with roles, capabilities, and personality
- [ ] No generic "Spial" text remaining anywhere on the site
- [ ] Each nav item links to its own separate page (not anchor sections)
- [ ] Blog lives on its own page
- [ ] Demo page showcasing the nonprofit user portal (dashboard preview)
- [ ] About page tells the Edify story (creative studio -> Edify OS)
- [ ] Site builds and deploys to Vercel without errors
- [ ] Design matches the current Spial template aesthetic (purple + dark navy)

## 4. Tech Stack

- **Language:** TypeScript
- **Framework:** Next.js 14 (static export)
- **Deployment:** Vercel (auto-deploy from main)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS (already in use)

## 5. Scope -- What is IN

### A. Landing Page Rewrite (page.tsx)
Keep the Spial template structure/design but replace ALL content:

1. **Hero Section** -- "Your nonprofit just hired an AI leadership team." Subtext about the problem (wearing too many hats, restricted funding, can't scale operations) and the solution (7 AI team members working around the clock). CTAs: "Start Free" + "See the Demo"

2. **The Problem** -- Brief section: nonprofits are mission-rich but resource-poor. Team members juggle 3-4 roles. Grant funding is restricted. You can't hire your way out. Edify OS is a different path.

3. **Meet Your AI Team** -- Card grid for all 7 archetypes with name, one-liner, 3 key capabilities, and link to detail page. Use role-specific accent colors.

4. **How It Works** -- 3-step flow: (1) Brief us on your mission (upload docs, describe programs), (2) Your team gets to work (proactive, autonomous), (3) Review, approve, lead (you stay in control)

5. **Features Deep Dive** -- Tabbed section (keep Spial tab design) but with real features: Grant Research & Writing, Donor Cultivation, Marketing & Communications, Financial Oversight, Program Design & Tracking, Volunteer Coordination, Event Planning

6. **Decision Lab Callout** -- "Run it by the team." 7 perspectives on every decision. Like having a leadership retreat in 30 seconds.

7. **Proactive Heartbeats Callout** -- Your team checks in on their own. Flags risks, celebrates wins, suggests next steps. No micromanagement.

8. **Stats Section** -- Nonprofit-relevant metrics (aspirational for now)

9. **Testimonials** -- Replace Spial testimonials with nonprofit-relevant quotes (placeholder names but real-sounding nonprofit use cases that match Edify's client profile: community orgs, cultural institutions, youth programs)

10. **FAQ Section** -- Rewrite all FAQs for Edify OS

11. **CTA Section** -- "Stop drowning in execution. Start leading."

12. **Footer** -- Edify OS branding with link to edifyanother.com, links to all pages

### B. Separate Pages (new routes)

1. `/about` -- The Edify story. Creative impact studio (edifyanother.com) that's worked with nonprofits for years. Saw the same pattern: brilliant missions, overwhelmed teams. Built Edify OS to fix it. Include: mission, origin story, connection to edifyanother.com, the team's nonprofit expertise.

2. `/features` -- Detailed feature breakdown per archetype. Each archetype gets a section with capabilities, example workflows, and what makes it different from generic AI chat.

3. `/pricing` -- Tier cards (pricing TBD, can use placeholder tiers for now)

4. `/blog` -- Blog listing page with 3-4 static placeholder articles relevant to nonprofits (e.g., "Why AI Won't Replace Your Team -- It'll Complete It", "The Grant Research Problem Nobody Talks About", "What Happens When Every Nonprofit Can Afford a Development Director")

5. `/contact` -- Contact info. Use Edify's contact: connect@edifyanother.com, (843) 929-4185, 500 Carteret St, Beaufort, SC 29902

6. `/integrations` -- Showcase supported integrations (Google Calendar, Gmail, Slack, social media, CRM, grant databases)

7. `/demo` -- **DEMO PAGE: Interactive preview of the nonprofit user portal.** This is key for pitching. Show annotated screenshots/mockups of:
   - The dashboard (team overview, recent activity)
   - Chatting with an archetype (e.g., asking the Dev Director to find grants)
   - Decision Lab in action (7 perspectives on a scenario)
   - Heartbeat updates in the inbox
   - The briefing/onboarding flow
   - This can be done as a guided visual walkthrough with styled screenshots of the actual dashboard pages, annotated with callouts explaining what each piece does.

### C. Navigation Update
- Navbar links point to separate pages (not /#anchors)
- Nav items: About, Features, Demo, Pricing, Blog, Contact
- "Meet the Team" or "Your AI Team" dropdown linking to the 7 archetype detail pages at /agents/*

### D. BYOK Dashboard Wiring (make the dashboard actually work)

The dashboard UI is fully built but all agent interactions go through API routes that proxy to a Python backend service (not deployed). Wire the frontend API routes to call Claude directly using the user's own API key (BYOK), eliminating the Python backend dependency for core functionality.

What needs to work end-to-end:

1. **Admin AI Config page** (`/dashboard/admin/ai-config`) -- User enters their Claude API key. Store in localStorage or Supabase (when available). This is the gate: nothing works without a key.

2. **Team Chat** (`/dashboard/team/[slug]`) -- Chatting with any of the 7 archetypes. Each archetype uses its system prompt from `services/agents/src/prompts/primary/*.md`. The API route at `/api/team/[slug]/chat` should call Claude Anthropic API directly with:
   - The archetype's system prompt
   - Conversation history from localStorage
   - The user's BYOK key from admin config

3. **Decision Lab** (`/dashboard/decision-lab`) -- Submit a scenario, get 7 archetype perspectives back. The API route at `/api/decision-lab` should fan out 7 parallel Claude calls (one per archetype), each with the archetype's system prompt + the scenario, then synthesize.

4. **Org Briefing** (`/dashboard/briefing`) -- The 4-step onboarding flow. Steps 1-3 collect info (org name, programs, goals). Step 4 uploads documents. Store org context in localStorage. This org context gets injected into all archetype system prompts.

5. **Conversation persistence** -- Use localStorage for now. Each archetype gets its own conversation history. Conversations sidebar works.

What stays on standby (needs funding):
- Stripe billing (pricing page shows tiers but no checkout)
- Supabase (auth uses localStorage mock, no real accounts)
- Python agent service (replaced by direct Claude calls)
- OAuth integrations (Google, social -- show UI but no real connections)
- Heartbeat scheduler (needs server-side cron)
- Document parsing (needs Python service)

## 6. Scope -- What is OUT

- No real blog CMS (just static placeholder articles)
- No real contact form submission (just UI)
- No Stripe checkout (pricing page is informational)
- No real OAuth connections (UI only)
- No heartbeat scheduling (needs server)
- No document parsing (needs Python)
- No changes to archetype detail pages at /agents/* (already good)

## 7. Key Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Reason |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Keep Spial design | Yes | Z likes the template aesthetic |
| Static pages only | Yes | No backend needed for marketing site |
| Placeholder testimonials | Yes | Real ones come after launch |
| Pricing TBD | Placeholder tiers | Final pricing not decided yet |
| Blog as static | Yes | No CMS needed yet |
| About = Edify, not Whitmore Labs | Yes | Edify is the client-facing brand |
| Demo page = visual walkthrough | Yes | Dashboard screenshots with annotations, not live demo |
| Contact info from edifyanother.com | Yes | Same team, same contact |

## 8. Deployment Plan

- **Repo:** whitmorelabs/edify-os (existing)
- **Deploy target:** Vercel (auto-deploy from main)
- **Domain:** edifyos.vercel.app (current)

## 9. Content Sources

The coding agent should pull real content from these files:
- `/ARCHETYPE_DESIGNS.md` -- Full archetype profiles, personalities, capabilities
- `/PRD-subagents-and-archetypes.md` -- Architecture and roles
- `/PRD-decision-lab.md` -- Decision Lab feature description
- `/PRD-proactive-heartbeats.md` -- Heartbeat system description
- `/PRD-user-guide.md` -- User onboarding flow
- `/content/guide/meet-your-team/*.md` -- Per-agent guide content
- Original landing page content from git history (commit 7050f97)
